Flat Capital's Board Must Seize Opportunity to Renegotiate Klarna Deal Terms - Di
data.description:
Aktiespararna's chief legal officer, Sverre Linton, believes that the investment company Flat Capital's board must address the opportunity to renegotiate the terms of the Klarna deal, if it has not already done so.

"I think this deal is still hanging by a rather thin thread," he told Dagens industri ahead of the extraordinary general meeting scheduled for next week.

Sebastian Siemiatkowski, major shareholder and chairman of Flat Capital, has faced a storm of criticism following the proposal to sell his Klarna shares at an inflated price to Flat Capital's shareholders. He has now indicated a willingness to sweeten the offer.

Dagens industri has calculated what price would be reasonable to accept.

When the deal was first presented, Siemiatkowski's Klarna shares were valued at $46.7 each, representing a certain premium over the market value.

"A fair arrangement would have looked different. Given that Flat shares were valued at 20 kronor, Klarna shares should have been valued at $39.75 per share. This would have meant roughly the same discount to the then-current share price as in Flat Capital, and would have landed marginally below Klarna's previous introduction price," Dagens industri writes.

Government Allocates Over 5 Billion SEK to Anti-Drone Systems and Enhanced Gripen Availability
data.description:
The Swedish government is investing more than 5 billion kronor to strengthen and accelerate the development of the Armed Forces' drone defense capabilities. This was announced by Defense Minister Pål Jonsson on the X platform.

The initiative aims to provide additional anti-drone systems and increase the availability of the Gripen fighter system. Pål Jonsson noted that recent airspace violations and drone sightings serve as a reminder that aerial threats are an increasingly significant aspect of modern warfare.

"More than 3.5 billion SEK will be invested in enhanced anti-drone capabilities between 2026 and 2028. This includes weapons to shoot down drones, interceptor drones for our air bases, and jamming sensors for both soldiers and vehicles. This will significantly boost our defense capabilities," the minister wrote, adding:

"Over 1.5 billion SEK will also be invested in combat aviation capabilities. We will purchase additional spare parts, mission equipment, and materials to enable operations at both air and road bases. This will improve the Gripen's availability and endurance in times of peace, crisis, and war."

Fast Retailing Shares Jump After Earnings Beat Expectations
data.description:
By Ronnie Harui


Fast Retailing's shares rose sharply on Friday after the Uniqlo owner reported stronger-than-expected annual results, driven by solid performances as its Japan and international units.

The stock was recently 6.3% higher at Y51,350.00 after climbing as much as 8.1% to Y52,190 yen, its highest intraday level since Jan. 9, 2025.

"The results underscored the firm's strong, stable earnings and operational know-how, so this should ease investors' jitters," said SMBC Nikko Securities' analyst Kuni Kanamori in a research note.

Uniqlo Japan's gross profit margin narrowed only slightly to 50.7% for the fiscal year ended August from 50.8% a year earlier. In North America, business profit margin widened by 1.2 percentage points to 16.3%, even as U.S. tariffs began to take effect.

While the market had been concerned about margin pressure at Uniqlo Japan and the potential impact of U.S. tariffs, the latest results showed positive signs, Kanamori said. Fast Retailing's guidance should also help dispel these concerns, he added.

For the current fiscal year that began in September, Fast Retailing projected revenue to rise 10% to Y3.750 trillion, operating profit to grow 8.1% to Y610.00 billion and net profit to edge up 0.5% to Y435.00 billion.


Write to Ronnie Harui at ronnie.harui@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-10-25 0054ET

Coinbase, Mastercard Reportedly Held Advanced Talks to Buy BVNK
data.description:
Coinbase Global, Inc. (NasdaqGS:COIN) and Mastercard Incorporated (NYSE:MA) have each held advanced acquisition talks to buy London-based BVNK (BVNK Digital Assets Limited), according to six sources familiar with the dealings, who asked for anonymity to talk about confidential business discussions. The terms and winning bidder haven?t been finalized, but the sale price is in the range of $1.5 billion to $2.5 billion, according to some of the sources. The talks may not result in a final deal, but at present Coinbase appears to have the inside track over Mastercard, three of the sources told Fortune.

If any deal is reached, it would be the largest stablecoin acquisition yet, and another signal that stablecoins, or cryptocurrencies pegged to underlying assets like the U.S. dollar, have reached the financial mainstream. And if BVNK decides to sell itself to Mastercard, it?s the clearest sign yet that the incumbent payments network?whose shares fell in June on news that Amazon and Walmart were pursuing stablecoins?is taking the rise of the technology seriously. BVNK, Mastercard, and Coinbase declined to comment.

data.title: Lovaglio: "Generali is Strategic, MPS Focused on Mediobanca"
data.description:
(Alliance News) - MPS CEO Luigi Lovaglio, speaking before the Senate Banking Commission, reiterated the importance of Generali, describing it as "a strategic and profitable asset" not tied to banking activities, according to La Repubblica on Friday.

Lovaglio explained that the 13.2% stake in the insurer offers opportunities in bancassurance, especially with the renegotiation of the agreement with AXA scheduled for 2026.

Following the OPAS on Mediobanca, which saw 86% participation, Lovaglio expressed confidence in the operation, which is supported by Delfin and Caltagirone. "Seventy percent of the capital is held by Italian and foreign funds: ours is a market operation," he clarified.

"The Mediobanca brand is sacred, just like that of MPS," he said, ruling out any departures of managers. On Banco BPM: "It is an excellent bank, but today we are focused on Mediobanca, where speed is essential."

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

EU Readies Action Against Italy Over Golden Power in UniCredit-BPM Case
data.description:
(Alliance News) - The dispute between UniCredit and Banco BPM may not be over yet, as reported by Il Corriere della Sera on Friday.

Citing Reuters, the European Commission is preparing to take action against Italian legislation on Golden Power, which it considers an obstacle to banking consolidation in Europe.

Brussels accuses the Meloni government of imposing restrictions that limit competition and violate the independence of banks. According to Il Corriere, two proceedings against Italy are expected by mid-November: one concerning the single market and another regarding mergers.

Already in July, the Commission had criticized the conditions set by Palazzo Chigi on UniCredit's offer for Banco BPM, deeming them incompatible with EU law.

The initiative, the newspaper continues, aims to encourage the creation of stronger European banking groups and also serves as a warning to Germany and Spain, where the Commerzbank and Sabadell cases remain unresolved.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Evolution Wins Legal Battle to Unmask Source Behind Short Seller Report
data.description:
Live casino developer Evolution has emerged victorious in its final legal battle to uncover the identity behind the 2021 short seller report that wiped out a significant portion of its market value.

The Supreme Court of the state of New Jersey, USA, has denied the Israeli firm Black Cube's request for appeal. This means Black Cube must either disclose the name of its secret client or face charges of contempt of court, according to the igaming site Next.io. Black Cube was responsible for compiling the report on behalf of the mysterious client.

Evolution has spent several years pursuing the name behind the report and has won every legal challenge along the way.

“I believe the world will eventually discover the truth, but it may take some time,” Evolution CEO Martin Carlesund told Next.io.

The report was released in November 2021 via the law firm Calcagni & Kanefsky, which acted as the public face of the report. The report accused Evolution of operating illegally in certain prohibited markets and of violating U.S. sanctions. The allegations damaged Evolution's reputation and resulted in tens of billions being wiped from its market capitalization.

Bloomberg News previously reported that a competitor of Evolution may have been responsible for the report, but this has not been confirmed.
